Q: If 1200 people are on the bus, and each person gets off at stations every 40 minutes, how many people are left after 2 hours?

A: First, calculate how many stations the bus covers in 2 hours: 2 hours * 15 minutes/station = 30 stations.
Then, determine how many people will get off at each station: 1200 people / 30 stations = 40 people/station.
So, the number of people left after 2 hours is 40.
